title: What everyone is saying about smaller teams in the age of AI
excerpt: Jeff Bezos famously lived by a two-pizza rule. Thanks to AI, the days of such larger teams may be over.
content_html:
Silicon Valley's next big idea is actually quite small.

Amazon founder Jeff Bezos famously suggested that to maintain productive meetings, everyone present should be able to be fed with two pizzas.

In the age of AI, teams across business and tech are shrinking. Some executives have said that's why they slashed jobs. Others just want more streamlined divisions.

It remains to be seen if OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's prediction of 10-person companies with billion-dollar valuations will come true.

In the meantime, there will likely be a lot fewer pizzas or a lot more leftovers.

Here's how corporate leaders are thinking about team size.

Adam Mosseri

Instagram CEO Adam Mosseri said his division has made "a very big shift" to scaling down the size of its average teams.

Before AI, Mosseri said the average team was roughly "a baker's dozen." Now, Instagram has pods, consisting of "four to six engineers who are a bit more generalists," a member of the product staff, and a specialized expert for whatever the team is working on.

"What's clearly happening is all of the functions are starting to bleed into each other, and the whole industry's wrestling with what that means," Mosseri said during an episode of "Lenny's Podcast" in July.

Mosseri said the leaner approach meant his teams "can often move faster and make better decisions, a little bit less designed by committee."

Mark Zuckerberg

Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said building the future of AI doesn't require large teams either.

"In order to make progress in AI, you don't need like many, many hundreds of AI researchers or thousands or anything like that," Zuckerberg said during an episode of the "No Priors" podcast in June. "I think you can really make progress with a very strong group of a dozen or a couple dozen people."

Jack Dorsey

Block CEO Jack Dorsey cited gains from AI as the reason he cut nearly half the staff at the fintech.

"We're already seeing that the intelligence tools we're creating and using, paired with smaller and flatter teams, are enabling a new way of working which fundamentally changes what it means to build and run a company," Dorsey wrote on X in February, explaining the decision.

Dorsey said he had two options when he considered the future of Block, the parent company of Square, Cash App, Afterpay, TIDAL, Bitkey, and Proto.

"I had two options: cut gradually over months or years as this shift plays out, or be honest about where we are and act on it now. I chose the latter," he wrote.

Brian Armstrong

Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong said the crypto giant would experiment with team size as it sought to "be leaner, faster, and more efficient for our next phase of growth."

Armstrong announced in May that Coinbase was laying off roughly 14% of its workforce.

"We'll be concentrating around AI-native talent who can manage fleets of agents to drive outsized impact," he wrote in a post outlining the move. "We'll also be experimenting with reduced pod sizes, including 'one person teams' with engineers, designers, and product managers all in one role."

Evan Spiegel

Snap CEO Evan Spiegel said the social network is already seeing the success of smaller teams.

"We have already witnessed small squads leveraging AI tools to drive meaningful progress across several important initiatives, including Snapchat+, enhanced ad platform performance, and efficiency improvements in our Snap Lite infrastructure," Spiegel wrote in an April letter.

At the time, Spiegel reiterated that Snap is "facing a crucible moment" requiring a new way of working that is "is faster and more efficient." Snap laid off roughly 16% of its full-time workforce.

Spiegel had previously laid out an ambitious plan for streamlined teams, which he compared to startups.

"Five to seven teams—squads of 10 to 15 people—will run like startups inside Snap, with single-threaded leaders accountable for outcomes," he wrote in a September 2025 letter. "Weekly demo days, 90-day mission cycles, and a culture of fast failure will keep us moving."

Avishai Abrahami

Wix CEO Avishai Abrahami said AI has sparked "the most significant shift in how companies are built since the invention of modern programming languages in the 1970s."

"It also means we need to become a faster, leaner, and flatter organization," Abrahami wrote on X in May.

Wix laid off roughly 20% of its workforce. Abrahami said the decision was due in part to a desire to reorganize the web development company.

"We are moving to a structure with fewer levels between any member of our leadership and the most junior person on the team," Abrahami wrote. "Fewer layers means faster decisions, clearer ownership, and less distance between the people setting direction and the people building the product - but it also means a smaller number of people."

Jamie Dimon

JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon said small, nimble teams should be able to operate like elite military special forces units.

"The teams needed to tackle these challenges should be small and authorized with the decision-making ability to move and act like Navy SEALs or the Army's Delta Force," Dimon wrote in his annual letter to shareholders, published in April.

While Dimon didn't call out AI specifically, he said teams need total buy-in from their members, so the groups can move at the "super speed" JPMorgan needs.

David Pan

Cursor field chief technology officer David Pan thinks it's time to revise Bezos' pizza rule.

"He was right about small teams," Pan wrote on X in July. "But in the AI era, two pizzas are too much pizza."

Pan praised "the all-time great metaphor," but said it was time to replace it.

"RIP to the two pizza team," he wrote.

Read next

Brent D. Griffiths

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Brent Griffiths is a senior reporter at Business Insider who covers AI and tech.Previously, he worked at the Washington Post as a researcher on Power Up and the Finance 202. He started his career at Politico where he worked on the web production team and covered breaking news. His passion for covering politics has only grown since he cut his teeth covering the presidential campaign as a student journalist. He's also contributed to the Almanac of American Politics.

  • AI
  • Leadership

sentences.en: I chose the latter," he wrote.
sentences.zh: 我选择了后者，”他写道。

sentences.zh: Wix 首席执行官 Avishai Abrahami 表示，人工智能引发了“自 20 世纪 70 年代现代编程语言发明以来，公司构建方式发生的最重大转变”。

blocks.html_en: "It also means we need to become a faster, leaner, and flatter organization," Abrahami wrote on X in May.

sentences.en: "It also means we need to become a faster, leaner, and flatter organization," Abrahami wrote on X in May.
sentences.zh: “这也意味着我们需要成为一个更快、更精简、更扁平的组织，”亚伯拉罕米五月份在 X 上写道。

blocks.html_en: Wix laid off roughly 20% of its workforce. Abrahami said the decision was due in part to a desire to reorganize the web development company.

sentences.en: Wix laid off roughly 20% of its workforce.
sentences.zh: Wix 解雇了大约 20% 的员工。

sentences.en: Abrahami said the decision was due in part to a desire to reorganize the web development company.
sentences.zh: 亚伯拉罕米表示，做出这一决定的部分原因是希望重组网络开发公司。

blocks.html_en: "We are moving to a structure with fewer levels between any member of our leadership and the most junior person on the team," Abrahami wrote. "Fewer layers means faster decisions, clearer ownership, and less distance between the people setting direction and the people building the product - but it also means a smaller number of people."

sentences.en: "We are moving to a structure with fewer levels between any member of our leadership and the most junior person on the team," Abrahami wrote.
sentences.zh: 亚伯拉罕米写道：“我们正在转向一种在我们的领导层成员和团队中资历最浅的人之间级别更少的结构。”

sentences.en: "Fewer layers means faster decisions, clearer ownership, and less distance between the people setting direction and the people building the product - but it also means a smaller number of people."
sentences.zh: “更少的层次意味着更快的决策、更清晰的所有权，以及设定方向的人和构建产品的人之间的距离更短——但这也意味着更少的人员数量。”

blocks.html_en: Jamie Dimon

sentences.en: Jamie Dimon
sentences.zh: 杰米戴蒙

sentences.en: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon SAUL LOEB / AFP via Getty Images
sentences.zh: 摩根大通首席执行官杰米·戴蒙 (Jamie Dimon) SAUL LOEB / 法新社 (Getty Images)

blocks.html_en: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon said small, nimble teams should be able to operate like elite military special forces units.

sentences.en: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon said small, nimble teams should be able to operate like elite military special forces units.
sentences.zh: 摩根大通首席执行官杰米戴蒙表示，小型、灵活的团队应该能够像精锐的军事特种部队一样运作。

blocks.html_en: "The teams needed to tackle these challenges should be small and authorized with the decision-making ability to move and act like Navy SEALs or the Army's Delta Force," Dimon wrote in his annual letter to shareholders, published in April.

sentences.en: "The teams needed to tackle these challenges should be small and authorized with the decision-making ability to move and act like Navy SEALs or the Army's Delta Force," Dimon wrote in his annual letter to shareholders, published in April.
sentences.zh: 戴蒙在 4 月份发表的年度致股东信中写道：“应对这些挑战所需的团队应该规模较小，并具有像海豹突击队或陆军三角洲部队那样行动和行动的决策能力。”

blocks.html_en: While Dimon didn't call out AI specifically, he said teams need total buy-in from their members, so the groups can move at the "super speed" JPMorgan needs.

sentences.en: While Dimon didn't call out AI specifically, he said teams need total buy-in from their members, so the groups can move at the "super speed" JPMorgan needs.
sentences.zh: 虽然戴蒙没有具体提到人工智能，但他表示团队需要成员的完全支持，这样团队才能以摩根大通所需的“超快速度”前进。

blocks.html_en: David Pan

sentences.en: David Pan
sentences.zh: 潘大卫

sentences.en: Cursor's website is seen on a laptop Gabby Jones/Bloomberg via Getty Images
sentences.zh: Cursor 的网站可以在笔记本电脑上看到 Gabby Jones/Bloomberg via Getty Images

blocks.html_en: Cursor field chief technology officer David Pan thinks it's time to revise Bezos' pizza rule.

sentences.en: Cursor field chief technology officer David Pan thinks it's time to revise Bezos' pizza rule.
sentences.zh: Cursor领域首席技术官David Pan认为是时候修改贝索斯的披萨规则了。

blocks.html_en: "He was right about small teams," Pan wrote on X in July. "But in the AI era, two pizzas are too much pizza."

sentences.en: "He was right about small teams," Pan wrote on X in July.
sentences.zh: “他关于小团队的看法是正确的，”潘七月份在 X 上写道。

sentences.en: "But in the AI era, two pizzas are too much pizza."
sentences.zh: “但在人工智能时代，两个披萨就太多了。”

blocks.html_en: Pan praised "the all-time great metaphor," but said it was time to replace it.

sentences.en: Pan praised "the all-time great metaphor," but said it was time to replace it.
sentences.zh: 潘赞扬了“有史以来最伟大的比喻”，但表示是时候更换它了。

blocks.html_en: "RIP to the two pizza team," he wrote.

sentences.en: "RIP to the two pizza team," he wrote.
sentences.zh: “安息两个披萨团队，”他写道。

blocks.html_en: Brent Griffiths is a senior reporter at Business Insider who covers AI and tech.Previously, he worked at the Washington Post as a researcher on Power Up and the Finance 202. He started his career at Politico where he worked on the web production team and covered breaking news. His passion for covering politics has only grown since he cut his teeth covering the presidential campaign as a student journalist. He's also contributed to the Almanac of American Politics.

sentences.en: Brent Griffiths is a senior reporter at Business Insider who covers AI and tech.Previously, he worked at the Washington Post as a researcher on Power Up and the Finance 202.

sentences.en: He started his career at Politico where he worked on the web production team and covered breaking news.

blocks.html_en: • AI
• Leadership

sentences.en: Leadership
sentences.zh: 领导

content_html_zh:
硅谷的下一个伟大想法实际上相当小。

亚马逊创始人杰夫·贝佐斯有一句著名的建议，为了保持会议富有成效，在场的每个人都应该能够吃到两份披萨。

在人工智能时代，跨业务和技术的团队正在缩小。 一些高管表示，这就是他们裁员的原因。 其他人只是想要更精简的部门。

OpenAI首席执行官Sam Altman关于10人公司估值数十亿美元的预测是否会成为现实，还有待观察。

与此同时，披萨的数量可能会少很多，或者剩菜的数量可能会多很多。

以下是企业领导者如何考虑团队规模。

亚当·莫塞里

Instagram 首席执行官 Adam Mosseri 表示，他的部门已经做出了“一个非常大的转变”，以缩小平均团队规模。

莫塞里表示，在人工智能出现之前，平均团队大约是“十几个面包师”。 现在，Instagram 有一个小组，由“四到六名多面手的工程师”、一名产品人员以及一名负责团队工作的专家组成。

“很明显，所有功能都开始相互渗透，整个行业都在努力思考这意味着什么，”Mosseri 在 7 月份的“Lenny's Podcast”一集中说道。

莫塞里表示，更精简的方法意味着他的团队“通常可以更快地行动并做出更好的决策，而不是由委员会设计。”

马克·扎克伯格

Meta 首席执行官马克·扎克伯格表示，构建人工智能的未来也不需要大型团队。

扎克伯格在 6 月份的“No Priors”播客节目中表示：“为了在人工智能方面取得进展，你不需要很多、数百名人工智能研究人员或数千名或类似的人。” “我认为，与一个由十几人或几十人组成的非常强大的团队一起，你真的可以取得进步。”

杰克·多尔西

Block 首席执行官杰克·多尔西 (Jack Dorsey) 将人工智能带来的收益作为他在金融科技公司裁员近一半的原因。

“我们已经看到，我们正在创建和使用的情报工具，与更小、更扁平的团队相结合，正在实现一种新的工作方式，从根本上改变了建立和运营公司的意义，”多西在二月份的 X 上写道，解释了这一决定。

Dorsey 表示，当他考虑 Square、Cash App、Afterpay、TIDAL、Bitkey 和 Proto 的母公司 Block 的未来时，他有两个选择。

“我有两个选择：随着这种转变的发生，在数月或数年内逐渐削减开支，或者诚实地了解我们所处的位置并立即采取行动。 我选择了后者，”他写道。

布赖恩·阿姆斯特朗

Coinbase 首席执行官布莱恩·阿姆斯特朗 (Brian Armstrong) 表示，这家加密巨头将尝试改变团队规模，因为它力求“在下一阶段的增长中变得更精简、更快、更高效”。

阿姆斯特朗 5 月份宣布，Coinbase 将裁员约 14%。

他在概述这一举措的帖子中写道：“我们将专注于人工智能原生人才，他们可以管理代理团队以产生巨大的影响。” “我们还将尝试缩小 Pod 的规模，包括由工程师、设计师和产品经理组成的‘一人团队’，他们都扮演一个角色。”

埃文·斯皮格尔

Snap 首席执行官埃文·斯皮格尔 (Evan Spiegel) 表示，该社交网络已经看到了小型团队的成功。

Spiegel 在 4 月份的一封信中写道：“我们已经见证了小团队利用 AI 工具推动多项重要举措取得有意义的进展，包括 Snapchat+、增强的广告平台性能以及 Snap Lite 基础设施的效率改进。”

当时，斯皮格尔重申，Snap“正面临严峻时刻”，需要一种“更快、更高效”的新工作方式。 Snap 解雇了大约 16% 的全职员工。

斯皮格尔此前曾为精简团队制定了一项雄心勃勃的计划，他将其与初创公司进行了比较。

他在 2025 年 9 月的一封信中写道：“五到七个团队（10 到 15 人组成的小队）将像 Snap 内部的初创公司一样运作，单线领导者对结果负责。” “每周的演示日、90 天的任务周期以及快速失败的文化将使我们不断前进。”

阿维赛·阿布拉米

Wix 首席执行官 Avishai Abrahami 表示，人工智能引发了“自 20 世纪 70 年代现代编程语言发明以来，公司构建方式发生的最重大转变”。

“这也意味着我们需要成为一个更快、更精简、更扁平的组织，”亚伯拉罕米五月份在 X 上写道。

Wix 解雇了大约 20% 的员工。 亚伯拉罕米表示，做出这一决定的部分原因是希望重组网络开发公司。

亚伯拉罕米写道：“我们正在转向一种在我们的领导层成员和团队中资历最浅的人之间级别更少的结构。” “更少的层次意味着更快的决策、更清晰的所有权，以及设定方向的人和构建产品的人之间的距离更短——但这也意味着更少的人员数量。”

杰米戴蒙

摩根大通首席执行官杰米戴蒙表示，小型、灵活的团队应该能够像精锐的军事特种部队一样运作。

戴蒙在 4 月份发表的年度致股东信中写道：“应对这些挑战所需的团队应该规模较小，并具有像海豹突击队或陆军三角洲部队那样行动和行动的决策能力。”

虽然戴蒙没有具体提到人工智能，但他表示团队需要成员的完全支持，这样团队才能以摩根大通所需的“超快速度”前进。

潘大卫

Cursor领域首席技术官David Pan认为是时候修改贝索斯的披萨规则了。

“他关于小团队的看法是正确的，”潘七月份在 X 上写道。 “但在人工智能时代，两个披萨就太多了。”

潘赞扬了“有史以来最伟大的比喻”，但表示是时候更换它了。

“安息两个披萨团队，”他写道。

布伦特·D. Griffiths 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

布伦特·格里菲斯 (Brent Griffiths) 是《商业内幕》(Business Insider) 的高级记者，报道人工智能和科技。此前，他曾在《华盛顿邮报》担任 Power Up 和 Finance 202 的研究员。 他的职业生涯始于 Politico，在网络制作团队工作并报道突发新闻。 自从他作为一名学生记者开始报道总统竞选以来，他对报道政治的热情与日俱增。 他还为《美国政治年鉴》做出了贡献。

  • 领导
